body.title: [sprint] PRE-EXISTING: 2 red in tests/inference/test_capability_evidence.py — a measured-capability claim is not backed by its measurement
body.description:
Found 2026-07-12 while running suites for T-agentic-primitives-package. VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING: both reproduce at HEAD in a clean worktree (2 failed, 6 passed), so NOT caused by the agentic move.

RED:
  test_a_measured_capability_claim_is_backed_by_its_measurement
  test_the_top_bucket_claim_is_backed_by_a_clean_pass_of_the_top_band

WHY THIS ONE IS NOT COSMETIC: these tests exist to enforce 'measure capability, never declare it' — a capability claim in the model registry must point at the measurement that backs it. Two red here means a claim is currently asserted WITHOUT its backing measurement, which is exactly the failure class the tests were written to catch (a tool-less model took the coding slot and confabulated completion — the unearned fold-back in D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations).

So: do NOT fix by relaxing the assertion. Either the measurement is missing (run it) or the claim is unearned (drop it).

**Test plan:** red->green: both nodes green with the claim backed by a real measurement record, not by weakening the guard.
